automotive-ev-battery.title: Stellantis plans 29 new electric vehicles and a new platform for all drive types
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Stellantis has unveiled its 'FaSTLAne 2030' strategic plan, committing €60 billion in investments over the next five years to accelerate growth and profitability across its four major brands. The plan focuses on developing 29 new electric vehicles and introducing STLA One, a new modular architecture designed to support multiple powertrain types.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Ganfeng starts pilot production of 500 Wh/kg solid-state batteries
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese battery specialist Ganfeng Lithium has commenced pilot production of lithium-metal solid-state batteries achieving 500 Wh/kg energy density, alongside reaching cycle life milestones for its 400 Wh/kg platform. This advancement represents significant progress in next-generation battery technology for electric vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Two in three new cars in Nordic countries are electric
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Nordic countries have achieved a historic milestone with two of three newly registered passenger vehicles in April being purely electric, driven by exceptional adoption rates in Norway at 98.6% and Denmark at 81.9%. Finland and Sweden recorded lower but still substantial electric vehicle adoption rates of 48.8% and 42% respectively.

automotive-ev-battery.title: First drive in the Kia EV2: Compact EV with a surprisingly mature setup
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia's new EV2 compact SUV demonstrates maturity and sophistication despite its 4.06-meter dimensions, offering efficient packaging and high comfort levels typically reserved for larger models. The vehicle's suitability for everyday use depends significantly on the correct configuration selection.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hanoi to get 1,000 battery-swapping stations
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hanoi has partnered with LG Energy Solution and Honda to establish a large-scale battery-swapping network for electric motorcycles, launching 50 pilot stations in 2026 before expanding to approximately 1,000 locations across the capital from 2027. This initiative addresses the critical infrastructure gap for electric two-wheelers in Southeast Asia.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Europe must close ranks on bidirectional charging as Germany dawdles
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Germany faces challenges in deploying bidirectional charging capabilities that would enable electric vehicle owners to support grid stability, with the German Ministry of Economic Affairs hosting the third European Summit on the topic. Progress from legacy automakers is materializing, though significant regulatory and technical hurdles remain before widespread vehicle-to-grid implementation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Netze BW launches real-world pilot for grid-friendly electric truck charging
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Netze BW, leveraging its established expertise in electric vehicle charging infrastructure, is conducting a real-world pilot project with logistics partners to optimize grid-friendly charging for commercial electric trucks. This initiative extends the distribution grid operator's proven approach from passenger vehicles to the commercial transportation sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Renewable Metals secures Series A funding
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Australian battery materials recycling startup Renewable Metals has raised $12 million AUD in Series A funding to advance its innovative battery recycling technology. The investment will support the development of a prototype plant in Western Australia and the design of its first commercial facility in New South Wales.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Germany launches new funding call for battery-electric buses
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Germany's Federal Ministry of Transport has announced a new funding programme supporting the procurement of approximately 1,500 battery-electric and fuel cell buses. The programme marks a shift in subsidy structure, reducing the funding rate from 80 percent of additional diesel-comparative costs to a lower percentage.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Stellantis to Build EVs for Dongfeng in France
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Stellantis is establishing European manufacturing capacity for Chinese automaker Dongfeng, continuing the trend of Chinese electric vehicle producers establishing production facilities in Europe. This partnership leverages Stellantis's existing French manufacturing infrastructure to serve both regional and broader market demand.

science-space.title: NASA Highlights 2025 International Space Station Science Results
science-space.summary: NASA released its 2025 Annual Highlights detailing comprehensive research achievements from the International Space Station, including over 750 investigations conducted throughout the year. The research advanced understanding of life in space, drove innovations benefiting people on Earth, and supported future exploration missions.

science-space.title: NASA Joins Artemis Accords Workshop as Global Signings Rise
science-space.summary: The United States participated in an Artemis Accords workshop in Lima, Peru, as six additional countries including Latvia, Jordan, Morocco, Malta, Ireland, and Paraguay joined the international coalition committed to safe and responsible lunar and planetary exploration. This expansion reflects growing global consensus on sustainable space exploration practices.

science-space.title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft's Orbit
science-space.summary: NASA will demonstrate a robotic servicing mission at Wallops Flight Facility where Northrop Grumman's Pegasus XL rocket will carry the Katalyst LINK spacecraft to rendezvous with and boost the orbit of the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory. This represents a significant advancement in on-orbit satellite servicing capabilities.

science-space.title: This exoplanet weather forecast by the James Webb Space Telescope calls for sandy skies and a clear (alien) sunset
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has provided unprecedented atmospheric observations of a distant hot Jupiter exoplanet, tracking cloud dissipation from morning through evening. This research demonstrates JWST's capability to characterize exoplanet atmospheres and weather patterns in remarkable detail.

science-space.title: NASA's AWE Completes Mission to Study Earth's Effect on Space Weather
science-space.summary: NASA's Atmospheric Waves Experiment instrument successfully completed its mission aboard the International Space Station after exceeding its planned two-year operational timeline. The instrument studied atmospheric gravity waves and their role in space weather dynamics from its installation in November 2023 through May 2026.

science-space.title: Lunar Outpost has big plans for the moon. The new Pegasus lunar rover is just the start
science-space.summary: Lunar Outpost unveiled designs for the Pegasus moon rover, scheduled for a 2028 launch, as the initial phase of an ambitious expansion of lunar surface capabilities. The Colorado-based company has outlined broader plans for sustained robotic and human operations on the lunar surface.

science-space.title: NASA Seeks Interest for Artemis Mission CubeSats
science-space.summary: NASA is soliciting proposals from organizations interested in deploying CubeSats on future Artemis missions, with a submission deadline of June 1. The opportunity allows researchers and technology developers to conduct investigations aboard the Space Launch System rocket in support of human space exploration expansion.

science-space.title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
science-space.summary: The Hubble Space Telescope has captured detailed imagery of an enigmatic galaxy displaying characteristics of spiral structure obscured by dust filaments and reddish-brown clumps. The observation provides insights into galactic morphology and the complex interplay between dust and stellar light in transitional galaxy systems.

science-space.title: Strange stacked stones spotted on Mars
science-space.summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover has discovered an unusual formation of stacked rocks on the Martian surface, an intriguing geological feature warranting further investigation. The discovery contributes to ongoing efforts to understand Mars' geological processes and surface characteristics.

science-space.title: Lunar Robots: NASA Spotlights Moon Base at 2026 FIRST Robotics Competition
science-space.summary: NASA highlighted the critical role of robotics in establishing long-term lunar presence during the 2026 FIRST Robotics World Championship, engaging students and industry professionals in future moon exploration technologies. The initiative aims to inspire the next generation of engineers and innovators to contribute to NASA's ambitious lunar development program.

tech-ai.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman's battle over OpenAI
tech-ai.summary: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could fundamentally alter the future of OpenAI and ChatGPT. Musk's lawsuit alleges that OpenAI abandoned its founding mission to develop AI for humanity's benefit in favor of profit maximization, with testimony from major tech leaders including Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella shaping the proceedings.

tech-ai.title: How Elon Musk will increase his power through the SpaceX IPO
tech-ai.summary: Elon Musk will maintain monarchical control over SpaceX following its planned public offering, retaining over 50% voting power—a level of control far exceeding what other tech founders typically retain in their publicly-traded companies. This unprecedented voting arrangement will give Musk decisive authority over corporate decisions regardless of other shareholders' interests.

tech-ai.title: Waymo expands pause to four cities as robotaxis keep driving into floods
tech-ai.summary: Waymo has expanded its service suspension to include Atlanta and San Antonio as the autonomous vehicle company works to resolve critical safety issues causing its robotaxis to navigate into flooded roadways. The multi-city pause highlights ongoing challenges in autonomous vehicle perception and decision-making systems under adverse weather conditions.

tech-ai.title: Spotify and Universal Music strike deal allowing fan-made AI covers and remixes
tech-ai.summary: Spotify has partnered with Universal Music Group to enable Premium subscribers to create AI-generated song covers and remixes, with participating artists receiving revenue shares from the new feature. This landmark deal represents a significant step in legitimizing AI-generated music within the streaming industry while protecting artist interests.

tech-ai.title: Firefox is working on a rounded redesign with easy-to-find controls for privacy and AI
tech-ai.summary: Mozilla is rolling out "Project Nova," a major visual overhaul of Firefox featuring rounded UI elements and a redesigned Settings section that prioritizes privacy controls and includes a dedicated switch for disabling present and future AI features. The redesign reflects growing user demands for transparent control over AI integration and enhanced privacy protections.

tech-ai.title: SpaceX scrubs first Starship V3 launch just before liftoff
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX cancelled its planned launch of the third-generation Starship rocket moments before liftoff after fueling the booster and spacecraft, with the company targeting a reattempt on Friday. The scrub marks another setback in SpaceX's accelerated development timeline for its next-generation heavy-lift vehicle.

tech-ai.title: Law enforcement shuts down VPN service used by two dozen ransomware gangs
tech-ai.summary: European law enforcement, working with Europol, has successfully dismantled First VPN, a service that promised hackers complete anonymity for cyberattacks but was used by approximately two dozen ransomware operations. The takedown demonstrates enhanced international coordination in disrupting criminal infrastructure and notifying affected users of their identification.

tech-ai.title: Anker's new earbuds are the first with its AI chip that boosts noise reduction
tech-ai.summary: Anker has launched the Liberty 5 Pro Max earbuds, the first models to feature its proprietary Thus AI chip designed to enhance noise reduction and voice clarity during calls in noisy environments. The earbuds also introduce a larger touchscreen interface and meeting recording capability without requiring a connected smartphone.

tech-ai.title: States ask judge to break up Live Nation-Ticketmaster
tech-ai.summary: More than 30 states are requesting that a federal judge mandate the breakup of Live Nation-Ticketmaster following an April jury verdict finding the company an illegal monopolist. The remedies proposal seeks forced divestiture of the ticketing giant, sale of major amphitheaters, and restrictions on its ability to tie venue access to its ticketing services.

tech-ai.title: Six search engines worth trying now that Google isn't really Google anymore
tech-ai.summary: As Google undergoes significant changes including expanded AI overview features, users seeking alternatives now have several viable search engine options to consider. The shift reflects growing user dissatisfaction with Google's direction and renewed interest in privacy-focused and feature-differentiated search competitors.

world-news.title: Iran steps up claim to control Strait of Hormuz
world-news.summary: Iran has published a map asserting "armed forces oversight" across more than 22,000 square kilometers of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical shipping chokepoints. The escalatory claim carries significant implications for global energy security and international maritime commerce.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Signs of progress amid efforts to reach US-Iran peace deal
world-news.summary: Negotiations between the United States and Iran show emerging signs of progress toward a potential peace agreement, with polling data indicating 60 percent of Americans now oppose further military escalation. The diplomatic developments represent a significant shift in one of the world's most consequential geopolitical standoffs.

world-news.title: Rubio says Cuba is threat to US as Havana accuses him of 'lies'
world-news.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has characterized Cuba as a threat to American national security, prompting Cuba's foreign minister to accuse him of attempting to "instigate military aggression." The escalating rhetorical conflict reflects deteriorating diplomatic relations in the Western Hemisphere.

world-news.title: Turkish court ousts leader of main opposition party
world-news.summary: An Ankara court has overturned the results of a leadership election won by Özgür Özel of Turkey's Republican People's Party, removing him from office in a contentious judicial decision. The ruling raises concerns about democratic processes and judicial independence within a NATO member state.

world-news.title: Board of Peace envoy warns 'permanent' Gaza divide under current status quo
world-news.summary: A UN peace envoy's board has warned that the continuation of current policies will result in permanent division in Gaza, calling on the UN Security Council to enforce Israeli obligations and Hamas disarmament. The assessment underscores persistent international concerns regarding the long-term viability of Middle East peace efforts.

world-news.title: Angry crowd sets Ebola hospital tents on fire in DR Congo
world-news.summary: Residents and relatives of an Ebola victim set fire to hospital tents in the Democratic Republic of Congo after being prevented from retrieving a body for traditional burial. The incident reflects growing public distrust of health authorities and complicates outbreak containment efforts in the region.

world-news.title: Gonorrhoea and syphilis hit record levels in Europe
world-news.summary: A European health agency reports record surges in gonorrhoea and syphilis cases, attributed to increased infections compounded by gaps in testing and disease prevention infrastructure. The crisis highlights deteriorating sexual health outcomes across the continent and calls for strengthened public health responses.

world-news.title: Gaza flotilla activists deported from Israel as backlash over treatment grows
world-news.summary: Israeli authorities have deported Gaza flotilla activists following international condemnation over a video depicting a far-right government minister taunting bound activists. The incident has intensified global criticism regarding treatment of humanitarian workers and Israel's approach to aid shipments.

world-news.title: US Senate pushes back against Trump's $1.8bn 'anti-weaponisation' fund
world-news.summary: The U.S. Senate has delayed a vote on a $1.8 billion Trump administration immigration enforcement fund, marking a rare instance of legislative resistance to executive priorities. The procedural setback reflects emerging tensions within the Republican-controlled chamber over the administration's policy agenda.

world-news.title: Air France and Airbus found guilty of manslaughter over 2009 plane crash
world-news.summary: A French court has convicted Air France and Airbus of manslaughter in connection with the 2009 crash of Flight AF447, which killed 228 people over the Atlantic Ocean. The historic ruling establishes corporate criminal liability for aviation disasters and carries implications for international aircraft safety standards.

finance-markets.title: 'Fast entry' SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs to ignite Wall St trading frenzy
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic are poised for rapid public listings that will trigger significant market activity as passive investors liquidate billions in shares to accommodate the new entries. The anticipated IPOs represent a major shift in the technology sector's capital markets landscape and are expected to generate substantial trading volumes on Wall Street.

finance-markets.title: Trump abruptly postpones AI order after White House infighting
finance-markets.summary: President Trump delayed signing a planned AI executive order amid internal White House disagreements over concerns that strict regulations could disadvantage American innovators relative to Chinese competitors. The postponement signals ongoing policy uncertainty in the artificial intelligence sector and reflects tensions within the administration regarding innovation versus regulation.

finance-markets.title: Goldman Sachs eclipses Morgan Stanley's Michael Grimes on SpaceX IPO
finance-markets.summary: Goldman Sachs has assumed a leading role in managing SpaceX's blockbuster IPO, diminishing the influence of celebrated tech banker Michael Grimes of Morgan Stanley who previously held sway over Elon Musk's major transactions. The shift reflects changing dynamics in tech banking relationships and potential impacts from Grimes' involvement with the Trump administration.

finance-markets.title: China's National Team Set to Cut ETF Stakes by 90% in First Half
finance-markets.summary: China's state-backed 'National Team' is expected to reduce its holdings in domestic equity-tracking exchange-traded funds by approximately 90% during the first half of 2026, according to Bloomberg Intelligence analysis. The substantial reduction could significantly impact liquidity and valuations in Chinese equity markets and signals a strategic shift in government market support.

finance-markets.title: BofA Sees Foreign Exodus from Indian Stocks Extending Into 2027
finance-markets.summary: Bank of America forecasts that foreign investor outflows from Indian equities will persist into 2027 as Asian artificial-intelligence leaders offer superior earnings growth at more attractive valuations. The prolonged exodus reflects a rotation in emerging market capital flows and changing investor preferences toward AI-focused opportunities in the region.

finance-markets.title: Tuas' $1.1 Billion M1 Deal Collapses Amid Regulator Probe
finance-markets.summary: Tuas Ltd.'s planned $1.1 billion acquisition of Singapore telecommunications operator M1 Ltd. has collapsed following regulatory authorities' decision to halt their review of the transaction. The deal's failure represents a significant setback in Singapore's telecom sector consolidation and reflects heightened regulatory scrutiny of major telecom mergers.

finance-markets.title: US quantum investment includes groups tied to Trump administration
finance-markets.summary: Beneficiaries of a $2 billion federal quantum investment initiative include entities linked to the Trump family and a company led by a Pentagon official, raising questions about allocation criteria. The distribution of quantum research funds reflects potential conflicts of interest within the administration's technology investment strategy.

finance-markets.title: Oil Rises After Three-Day Drop With Iran-US Talks in Focus
finance-markets.summary: Crude oil prices recovered from a three-day decline as geopolitical developments regarding Iran-US negotiations and Iranian statements on uranium enrichment and the Strait of Hormuz influenced market sentiment. Energy markets remain volatile as diplomatic progress remains uncertain and could significantly impact global oil supply dynamics.

finance-markets.title: Global buyout funds to exit China's data centres with final $1bn deal
finance-markets.summary: Foreign private equity firms are completing their retreat from China's data centre sector with Princeton Digital Group's final $1 billion sale, marking the end of significant foreign involvement in the country's sensitive digital infrastructure. The divestments underscore growing geopolitical tensions and restrictions on foreign capital in China's strategic technology assets.

finance-markets.title: Tycoon Plows Billions Into EVs as His Vietnam Stock Booms 1,000%
finance-markets.summary: Vietnam's wealthiest individual has invested billions of personal capital into his emerging electric vehicle company and related ventures, with his corporate group's shares surging approximately 1,000 percent. The dramatic stock appreciation reflects investor enthusiasm for Vietnam's EV sector and the tycoon's strategic positioning in the region's electric mobility transition.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Closely watched experimental Parkinson's drug fails key clinical trial
health-biotech.summary: Biogen and Denali Therapeutics announced that their experimental Parkinson's disease therapy failed to meet primary endpoints in a randomized clinical trial, failing to slow disease progression. The setback represents a significant disappointment for a closely watched treatment candidate in neurodegenerative disease.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Merck-Kelun lung cancer drug cut risk of tumor progression by 65%, ASCO abstract shows
health-biotech.summary: A targeted chemotherapy antibody-drug conjugate developed by China-based Kelun-Biotech and licensed to Merck demonstrated a 65% reduction in tumor progression risk in Phase 3 lung cancer trials. The positive results represent a significant clinical advance in oncology and validate the partnership approach between Chinese and Western pharmaceutical companies.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We're reading about a Lilly obesity drug trial, statistics for an Alzheimer's drug, and more
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly reported that its next-generation obesity drug achieved weight loss outcomes approaching the effectiveness of bariatric surgery in late-stage clinical trials. The result positions Lilly's candidate among the leading obesity treatment options under development.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Immunovant shares surge on arthritis trial data
health-biotech.summary: Immunovant's stock rallied following positive trial results for Lilly's triple-G autoimmune disease drug candidate. The surge reflects investor confidence in the emerging class of triple agonist therapies for rheumatoid arthritis treatment.

health-biotech.title: How a group of biotechs ran a covert campaign to push out the FDA commissioner
health-biotech.summary: A consortium of biotechnology companies, including Biohaven, orchestrated a coordinated effort to pressure FDA Commissioner Marty Makary following the rejection of a spinocerebellar ataxia drug. The campaign highlights tensions between industry stakeholders and federal regulatory leadership.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: 3 burning questions senators had for the NIH director
health-biotech.summary: Senate appropriations committee members questioned NIH Director Jay Bhattacharya on agency leadership, recent viral outbreaks, and the pace of research funding distribution. The hearing reflects congressional oversight concerns regarding budget management and institutional readiness.

health-biotech.title: #ASCO26: Key head and neck cancer readouts from J&J, Corbus, Summit, Akero and Bicara
health-biotech.summary: Multiple pharmaceutical companies presented clinical data at ASCO for head and neck cancer treatments, addressing the challenge that these tumors suppress immune responses through multiple biological pathways. The readouts highlight the complexity of developing effective combination immunotherapy approaches for this indication.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Elevance executive ordered to testify in Medicare Advantage fraud case
health-biotech.summary: The Department of Justice has compelled an Elevance Health executive to provide testimony in a fraud investigation alleging the company fraudulently billed the government for inaccurate medical diagnoses. The case underscores ongoing government scrutiny of health insurance company billing practices.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Acting head of NIH's infectious disease institute reported to have stepped down
health-biotech.summary: Jeffery Taubenberger, the acting director of the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID), has reportedly stepped down from his position. The departure creates a leadership transition at a key NIH institute responsible for infectious disease research and policy.

health-biotech.title: BioMarin to seek further approval of Voxzogo in form of short stature
health-biotech.summary: BioMarin Pharmaceutical announced plans to seek expanded regulatory approval for its bone growth-stimulating therapy Voxzogo following positive results demonstrating improved growth rates in children with specific forms of short stature. The expansion represents a label extension opportunity for the established treatment.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto prediction markets are turning into dangerous national security risks, and Congress wants to ban them
crypto-web3.summary: Investigators discovered 80 bets on Polymarket with a statistically impossible 98% win rate, raising concerns about market manipulation and national security implications. Congressional action is being considered to regulate or ban crypto-based prediction markets.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum's identity crisis is deepening after high-profile 'brain drain' frustrates the community
crypto-web3.summary: Multiple departures of core Ethereum developers have triggered broader existential concerns within the community about the blockchain's future direction and leadership. The exits signal potential structural challenges facing the second-largest cryptocurrency platform.

crypto-web3.title: Blockchain.com files with SEC for U.S. IPO
crypto-web3.summary: The cryptocurrency financial services firm submitted a confidential S-1 filing to pursue a public listing as digital asset markets gain renewed institutional momentum. This move represents a significant milestone for crypto platform maturation and mainstream acceptance.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. CFTC secures deal with National Hockey League on prediction market safeguards
crypto-web3.summary: The derivatives regulator established a memorandum of understanding with the NHL following a similar agreement with Major League Baseball to implement safeguards in sports-based prediction markets. The arrangement reflects regulatory efforts to manage risks in the rapidly expanding prediction market sector.

crypto-web3.title: Satoshi's 1.1 million bitcoin and millions more can be saved from quantum attack, says expert
crypto-web3.summary: Privacy-focused blockchain researchers claim to have developed a multi-layer quantum defense mechanism that would protect dormant Bitcoin holdings through a soft fork protocol. The proposal addresses a long-standing concern about cryptocurrency vulnerability to future quantum computing threats.

crypto-web3.title: MoonPay expands into tokenized assets and DeFi markets with new platform for banks
crypto-web3.summary: MoonPay launched MoonPay Trade, a new infrastructure platform enabling banks and fintechs to access stablecoins, tokenized funds, and yield-generating products through a unified interface. The expansion reflects growing institutional demand for comprehensive digital asset solutions.

crypto-web3.title: Boerse Stuttgart adds Societe Generale, SG-FORGE, and flatexDEGIRO to 'pan-European' tokenized settlement network
crypto-web3.summary: Nasdaq's European trading venues are expanding the Seturion tokenized settlement network to include major financial institutions, facilitating cross-border trading and settlement of tokenized securities. The development demonstrates European financial infrastructure's commitment to blockchain-based asset settlement.

crypto-web3.title: Mark Cuban says he sold most of his Bitcoin after failed hedge narrative 'disappointed' the billionaire
crypto-web3.summary: The billionaire investor divested most of his Bitcoin holdings after concluding the cryptocurrency failed to provide portfolio hedging benefits during recent geopolitical turmoil and dollar weakness. The sale signals potential disappointment among prominent Bitcoin advocates regarding the asset's macroeconomic properties.

crypto-web3.title: Peer-to-peer trading startup Variational raises $50 million for real-world perps in funding round led by Dragonfly
crypto-web3.summary: The derivative trading platform secured $50 million in funding to expand its perpetual futures offerings on real-world assets including commodities such as oil, silver, copper, and gold. The investment underscores growing institutional interest in blockchain-based derivatives tied to traditional asset classes.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase introduces thematic perps tracking China, AI and US national security equity indexes
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase launched perpetual futures contracts based on MarketVector's sector-specific indexes tracking the largest companies in artificial intelligence, defense, and China-focused equities. The offering enables crypto traders to gain exposure to geopolitical and technology-driven market themes.

startups-vc.title: Beauty booking startup Fresha hits $1B valuation with KKR backing
startups-vc.summary: Beauty and wellness booking marketplace Fresha has achieved unicorn status with an $80 million Series growth investment from KKR's Next Generation Technology Growth fund. The milestone represents significant validation for the marketplace platform serving the beauty and wellness sector.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Anduril Leads Varied Lineup Of Large Deals
startups-vc.summary: Defense tech unicorn Anduril Industries dominated the week's fundraising activity with a $5 billion financing round, leading a diverse slate of major rounds across physical-world applications including robotics, space tech, and biotech. The variety of large deals reflects strong investor appetite across multiple emerging sectors.

startups-vc.title: General Catalyst just led a $63M bet on India's travel payments market
startups-vc.summary: Indian fintech startup Scapia, which integrates travel booking with co-branded credit cards and mobile payments, has doubled its valuation following a $63 million funding round led by General Catalyst. The investment highlights growing venture capital focus on emerging market payment solutions and travel fintech innovation.

startups-vc.title: Maka Kids is redefining kids' screen time with a streaming app optimized for well-being, not engagement
startups-vc.summary: Maka Kids has secured $3 million in seed funding to expand its streaming platform designed for children ages zero to six with content optimized for healthy development rather than engagement maximization. The funding reflects growing investor interest in responsible technology solutions for young audiences.

startups-vc.title: How Anthropic Rebuilt Its Sales Org From Scratch When Demand Went Vertical: 54% of New Enterprise Logos Now Come Self-Serve
startups-vc.summary: Following the December 2025 release of Claude Opus 4.6, Anthropic experienced explosive demand growth that forced rapid restructuring of its commercial organization, with over half of new enterprise customers now arriving through self-serve channels. The case study illustrates both the opportunities and operational challenges of managing hypergrowth in enterprise AI adoption.

startups-vc.title: The SpaceX IPO Filing Looks Nothing Like Those Of The Elite Group Of Tech Giants It's Hoping To Join
startups-vc.summary: SpaceX filed its public IPO prospectus this week, positioning itself for a trillion-dollar-plus valuation, with financial metrics and business characteristics notably distinct from other mega-cap tech companies that achieved similar valuations. The filing provides insight into how next-generation technology companies compare to the previous generation of tech giants at comparable stages.

startups-vc.title: This young startup is taking on a fragrance industry that hasn't changed in almost half a century
startups-vc.summary: Fragrance technology company Patina announced a $2 million seed round from investors including Betaworks and True Ventures to develop new approaches to fragrance molecule discovery. The funding signals emerging venture interest in applying technology innovation to traditionally static consumer product categories.

startups-vc.title: You don't need to be an AI startup to raise. Lucra has $20M to prove it.
startups-vc.summary: Lucra, an eSports gamification and loyalty platform, raised $20 million from Cathie Wood's ARK Invest despite not emphasizing artificial intelligence in its pitch, challenging the prevailing assumption that AI branding is essential for fundraising success. The round demonstrates that compelling business models in emerging sectors can still attract major venture capital without AI positioning.

startups-vc.title: Imperagen raises £5 million to use quantum physics, AI on enzyme engineering
startups-vc.summary: Biotech company Imperagen has closed a £5 million seed round led by PXN Ventures to advance enzyme engineering through quantum physics and artificial intelligence applications. The funding reflects growing investor confidence in computational biology approaches to address enzyme design challenges.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Quantum Computing Startup Investment Slows In 2026 While Public Markets Hold Strong
startups-vc.summary: Quantum computing startup funding is on track to decline from 2025 peaks, though deal counts remain robust and significant rounds continue to be deployed, indicating a potential market correction after previous years of elevated investment. The trend suggests investor sentiment is shifting toward more selective quantum technology bets with clearer commercial timelines.

cybersecurity.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
cybersecurity.summary: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal h

cybersecurity.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
cybersecurity.summary: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

cybersecurity.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
cybersecurity.summary: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

cybersecurity.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
cybersecurity.summary: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

cybersecurity.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at least mid-2022.

"Showboat is a modular post-exploitation framework designed for Linux systems, capable of spawning a remote shell, transferring files, and functioning as a SOCKS5 proxy," Lumen

cybersecurity.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories
cybersecurity.summary:
This week starts small.

A token leaks. A bad package slips in. A login trick works. An old tool shows up again. At first, it feels like the usual mess. Then you see the pattern: attackers are not always breaking in. They are using the parts we already trust.

That is what makes it worrying. The danger is in normal things now - updates, apps, cloud buttons, support chats, trusted accounts. AI

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
cybersecurity.summary:
Microsoft has disclosed that a privilege escalation and a denial-of-service flaw in Defender has come under active exploitation in the wild.

The former, tracked as CVE-2026-41091, is rated 7.8 on the CVSS scoring system. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges.

"Improper link resolution before file access ('link following') in Microsoft Defender

cybersecurity.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
cybersecurity.summary: Consider a cached access key on a single Windows machine. It got there the way most cached credentials do - a user logged in, and the key stored itself automatically. Standard AWS behavior. No one misconfigured anything or violated a policy. Yet that single key, which was easily accessible to a minor-league attacker, could have opened a path to some 98% of entities in the company's cloud

cybersecurity.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46333 (CVSS score: 5.5), is a case of improper privilege management that could permit an unprivileged local user to disclose sensitive files and execute arbitrary commands as root on default installations of several major

cybersecurity.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension
cybersecurity.summary:
GitHub on Wednesday officially confirmed that the breach of its internal repositories was the result of a compromise of an employee device involving a poisoned version of the Nx Console Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension. 

The development comes as the Nx team revealed that the extension, nrwl.angular-console, was breached after one of its developers' systems was hacked in the

energy-climate.title: Factcheck: US and Iran are world's only major emitters without net-zero targets
energy-climate.summary: A Carbon Brief analysis confirms that the United States and Iran remain the only major global emitters without officially committed net-zero emissions targets, challenging assertions by right-leaning figures that other nations lack climate commitments. The finding underscores significant divergence in climate policy leadership among the world's largest economies.

energy-climate.title: IRENA proposes global electrification target of 35% by 2035
energy-climate.summary: The International Renewable Energy Agency has released a report proposing a 35% global electrification target by 2035, emphasizing renewable energy's critical role in meeting rising electricity demand. The report highlights that with 2.5 TW of solar, storage, and wind projects already in connection queues worldwide, significant investment in grid infrastructure will be essential to achieve this goal.

energy-climate.title: European PV avoided €10 billion in gas imports since escalation of Iran conflict
energy-climate.summary: According to SolarPower Europe, European photovoltaic installations have generated sufficient electricity since early March to offset approximately €10 billion in gas imports, averaging €110 million in daily savings. The data demonstrates the tangible economic and strategic benefits of solar deployment in reducing energy import dependency.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: UK no longer top UN Green Climate Fund donor after latest aid cut
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief analysis reveals that the United Kingdom has relinquished its position as the leading contributor to the United Nations' Green Climate Fund following recent reductions in climate financing commitments. This shift reflects changing priorities in international climate finance leadership among developed nations.

energy-climate.title: Solar industry cost-cutting sparks record wave of spontaneous glass breakage
energy-climate.summary: The solar industry is experiencing a record surge in spontaneous glass breakage incidents in photovoltaic modules, a problem that has emerged over the past five years amid cost-cutting pressures. Manufacturing pressures and the absence of comprehensive industry standards are hampering efforts to develop effective solutions to this emerging quality issue.

energy-climate.title: New South Wales opens Tender 8 for 2.5GW of renewable capacity
energy-climate.summary: New South Wales has launched its largest renewable energy tender to date, seeking proposals for 2.5 gigawatts of new renewable infrastructure capacity. This significant procurement initiative signals accelerated renewable energy deployment in Australia's most populous state.

energy-climate.title: Antora, POET commission 5GWh thermal battery system in South Dakota
energy-climate.summary: Antora Energy and POET have commissioned a 5-gigawatt-hour thermal energy storage system at POET's bioprocessing facility in South Dakota, advancing long-duration energy storage capabilities. The deployment demonstrates the growing commercial viability of thermal battery technology for industrial applications.

energy-climate.title: Utility-scale solar expansion has little impact on U.S. crop prices
energy-climate.summary: Research from Indiana University indicates that concerns about utility-scale solar development's impact on agricultural production are largely overstated, with crop price increases expected below 5.6% for maize, soybeans, and wheat under historical expansion patterns. The findings suggest solar and agricultural land uses can coexist with minimal economic disruption to farming sectors.

energy-climate.title: UNSW researchers shed new light on UV-induced degradation in PERC, TOPCon solar cells
energy-climate.summary: University of New South Wales researchers have identified that ultraviolet exposure in PERC and TOPCon solar cells increases interface defects and recombination activity, causing degradation primarily on front and rear surfaces of PERC cells and front surfaces of TOPCon cells. The findings provide insights into material durability and inform design improvements for next-generation photovoltaic technologies.

energy-climate.title: ASL selects AGL, Enel X for 532MW of new NSW firming capacity
energy-climate.summary: AusEnergy Services Limited has contracted two new firming projects in New South Wales with combined capacity of 532 megawatts, partnering with AGL and Enel X to enhance grid reliability. The projects address growing demand for dispatchable capacity to support renewable energy integration in the Australian electricity system.

asia-pacific.title: Boeing went to China to sell planes. Beijing is buying something else
asia-pacific.summary:
When Air Force One touched down in Beijing, one of the most photographed passengers was Boeing’s chief executive, Kelly Ortberg, there to close a deal. Washington had come to sell aircraft. Beijing was buying something else.
The last time this scene played out in 2017, China signed an agreement for 300 Boeing aircraft. Since then, it has pursued an industrial programme aimed at reducing dependence on precisely such orders. That effort has not failed, but neither has it advanced at the pace...

asia-pacific.title: ‘We really do not trust each other’: former US envoy foresees wary new phase in China ties
asia-pacific.summary:
Former US ambassador to China Max Baucus has described the latest US-China summit as ushering in a new phase of wary “constructive stability”, with both sides focused more on preventing crises rather than building trust or meaningfully resetting their relationship.
In an interview on Wednesday, Baucus said last week’s Beijing summit between President Xi Jinping and his US counterpart Donald Trump served as a guard rail to prevent breakdown and escalation, while revealing the limits of the...

asia-pacific.title: China is losing the LLM race but it can still win in AI, ex-Tencent AI lead says
asia-pacific.summary:
For years, Liu Wei was synonymous with Chinese tech giant Tencent Holdings’ artificial intelligence efforts. A distinguished scientist at the Shenzhen-based company, Liu was also head of its Hunyuan team, the firm’s foundational model development unit for the generative AI era.
But in late 2024, Liu’s departure from Tencent after more than eight years sparked immediate speculation as to why he left. Hunyuan was introduced only a year earlier – so why did Liu suddenly quit one of China’s most...

asia-pacific.title: China inventor creates sword-shaped aerial vehicle, bringing flying sword in fantasy novels to life
asia-pacific.summary:
A Chinese influencer has turned the flying sword in Chinese xianxia fantasy novels into reality by creating a specially-shaped aerial vehicle.
Fan Shisan, from southwestern China’s Sichuan province, recently posted a video of himself riding a sword-shaped aerial vehicle, amassing 3.3 million views and 284,000 likes.
Dressed in black and striking a cool pose, Fan looks like a knight in China’s xianxia dramas.

Xianxia is a popular genre of Chinese fantasy developed from the wuxia, or martial...

asia-pacific.title: Northern Metropolis industrial park draws ‘global interest’ from AI, green firms
asia-pacific.summary:
The new industrial park under the Northern Metropolis megaproject has attracted strong global interest from companies in environmental and AI sectors, its chairman has said, with plans to provide international education and accommodation facilities.
Jeffrey Lam Kin-fung, the newly appointed chairman of the Hung Shui Kiu Industry Park Company, also aims to have the park operational by July next year, describing it as a “nice gift” for Hong Kong’s 30th anniversary of its return to Chinese...

asia-pacific.title: Robots at Singapore’s AI zone to clean, patrol and deliver goods
asia-pacific.summary:
As robots become more advanced to carry out complex tasks, they are set to be at the forefront of the next phase of Singapore’s goal to become a world-class artificial intelligence hub.
The city state hopes to position itself as a hub for “physical AI” – robots and autonomous systems that can clean buildings, deliver goods, patrol public spaces and eventually work in factories, hospitals and homes.
Singapore’s stable regulatory environment, strong digital infrastructure and experience deploying.

asia-pacific.title: Woman jailed for nearly 42 years over US$250 million Minnesota fraud case
asia-pacific.summary:
A judge on Thursday handed down an extraordinary prison sentence – nearly 42 years – to the former leader of a Minnesota charity who was convicted in a staggering US$250 million fraud case that helped ignite an immigration crackdown by the Trump administration.
Aimee Bock ran Feeding Our Future, which had claimed it helped provide millions of meals to children in need during the coronavirus pandemic.
“I understand I failed. I failed the public, my family, everyone,” Bock said in federal...

asia-pacific.title: 16 dead after gunmen open fire in 2 separate attacks in Honduras
asia-pacific.summary:
Gunmen opened fire in two separate attacks on Thursday on the Honduran coast, killing at least 16 people, including six police officers, police said.
The first incident took place at a plantation in the municipality of Trujillo in northern Honduras, where at least 10 workers were shot and killed, National Police spokesman Edgardo Barahona said.
The resource-rich region has been the site of a decades-long agrarian conflict.
The Inter-American Commission on Human Rights has previously issued...

asia-pacific.title: AI boom fuels US$11.6b data centre investment in Asia-Pacific as Hong Kong eyes niche role
asia-pacific.summary:
The artificial intelligence boom is partially spurring investment in Asia-Pacific’s data centre properties with a record US$11.6 billion worth of capital poured into the segment in 2025, according to CBRE.
In Hong Kong, while AI-related demand is unlikely to be met because of technical limitations, leasing of data centre space was supported by “hyperscale CSPs (cloud service providers), mainland Chinese technology and e-commerce companies and financial institutions”, the property consultancy...
